product_name: Union Bank of India Fixed Deposit

interest_rates.note: Senior citizens get an additional 0.50%. Rates change with RBI policy updates.

333_days.note: Union Bank Special FD — Limited period offer

deposit_limits.maximum: No upper limit (DICGC insurance covers up to ₹5 Lakh)

types_of_fd.name: Regular Fixed Deposit
types_of_fd.description: Standard FD, interest paid quarterly or at maturity
types_of_fd.premature_withdrawal: Allowed with 1% penalty on applicable rate

types_of_fd.name: Tax Saving FD (Section 80C)
types_of_fd.description: 5-year lock-in, up to ₹1.5 Lakh deduction under Section 80C
types_of_fd.premature_withdrawal: NOT allowed — cannot be broken before 5 years

types_of_fd.name: Union Samriddhi FD
types_of_fd.description: Special tenure FD — higher interest rate

types_of_fd.name: Flexi Fixed Deposit
types_of_fd.description: Linked to savings account, auto-sweep facility
types_of_fd.premature_withdrawal: Allowed without penalty for sweep portions

types_of_fd.name: Senior Citizen FD
types_of_fd.description: For customers aged 60 and above, 0.50% extra interest
types_of_fd.special_benefit: Also suggest SCSS (Senior Citizen Savings Scheme)

types_of_fd.name: NRE / NRO Fixed Deposit
types_of_fd.tax: NRE: Tax-free in India | NRO: Taxable

documents_needed.existing_customer: If KYC is already updated, no additional documents are needed

loan_against_fd.benefit: No need to break the FD — take a loan and keep the FD intact

auto_renewal.default: Auto-renewal occurs at the same tenure upon maturity
auto_renewal.customer_instruction: Customer can provide advance instructions for maturity disposition

premature_withdrawal.penalty: 1% less than applicable rate for actual tenure
premature_withdrawal.example: If a 1-year FD is broken at 6 months — the 6-month rate minus 1% will apply

tds_rules.threshold: TDS is deducted if interest exceeds ₹40,000 (₹50,000 for senior citizens)
tds_rules.exemption: Submit Form 15G (general) / Form 15H (senior citizen) to avoid TDS — if income is below taxable limit

process_steps.title: Understand Customer Goal
process_steps.detail: How long do they want to invest? Do they need monthly income or lump sum at maturity? Do they need tax saving?

process_steps.title: Suggest Best FD Type
process_steps.detail: Based on the goal — recommend Tax Saving FD, Special FD (333/456 days), or Regular FD

process_steps.title: Verify KYC
process_steps.detail: Existing customer = KYC already done. New customer = collect PAN + Aadhaar

process_steps.title: Confirm Amount & Tenure
process_steps.detail: Show the maturity amount using the calculator — exact returns

process_steps.title: Open the FD
process_steps.detail: At branch counter / via Net Banking / via Mobile App — suggest the most convenient option to the customer

process_steps.title: FD Receipt & Form 15G/H
process_steps.detail: Issue FD receipt. If customer's income is below tax bracket — have them fill Form 15G (under 60) or 15H (senior citizen)

staff_tips: If the customer is a senior citizen, the 0.50% extra rate applies automatically — remind them
333-day or 456-day special FDs offer the highest rates — suggest these first
If investing more than ₹5 Lakh, inform about the DICGC insurance limit
Also mention the loan against FD option — no need to break the FD in an emergency
Tax saving FD — ensure the customer understands the 5-year lock-in period
